Layout input: 150.0 mm x 90.0 mm rectangular prototype outline; origin at lower-left viewed from component side; battery/high-current entry edge is X=0; low-current CAN/service edge is X=150.0; target PCB thickness is 1.6 mm unless stackup review changes it
Engineering decision: Use the reviewed 150 mm x 90 mm prototype envelope as the first PB-100 board-import outline while enclosure CAD remains a later manufacturing-package input
Risk: Final enclosure CAD may force a respin; board area cost rises versus a smaller outline but gives high-current copper and service spacing margin
Manufacturing boundary: No Gerbers drill output pick-place BOM/CPL manufacturing ZIP fabrication package or PCBA order is authorized

Layout input: Four M3 clearance NPTH holes; finished hole diameter 3.2 mm; hole centers X6.0/Y6.0 X144.0/Y6.0 X6.0/Y84.0 X144.0/Y84.0; reserve 5.0 mm radius mechanical courtyard around each hole
Engineering decision: Use four-corner M3 retention because PB-100 carries high-current harness loads and needs service-cover stiffness independent of LB-100
Risk: Standoff pattern may move after enclosure CAD; keep holes NPTH and outside high-current copper corridors to preserve safety clearance
Manufacturing boundary: No drill files or fabrication outputs are generated from this planning input

Layout input: Keep the battery input as an off-board fused harness entry on the X=0 short edge; reserve X0.0..X25.0 and Y30.0..Y60.0 as the high-current cable strain-relief and input transition corridor; near-battery main fuse remains off-board
Engineering decision: Preserve the 50 A near-battery fuse and off-board harness boundary instead of adding a PCB-mounted battery connector in Rev.1
Risk: Exact gland boot and strain-relief hardware can change the enclosure opening; thermal impact depends on input copper width and return-path review
Manufacturing boundary: No cable gland drawing enclosure CAD or PCBA order is authorized

Layout input: Reserve high-current output harness exit zones for OUT1 and OUT2 along the lower long edge Y=0 with keepout windows X35.0..X65.0 and X85.0..X115.0; DTP mated housings remain garage harness parts outside the PCB outline
Engineering decision: Use off-board DTP harness exits for OUT1 and OUT2 so PCB layout can focus on MOSFET fuse shunt and copper geometry without locking connector shells onto PB-100
Risk: DTP shell boot and cable bend radius can force edge movement; high-current loop area must still close in thermal/current layout model
Manufacturing boundary: No connector-panel CAD harness drawing fabrication package or pick-place output is authorized

Layout input: Reserve medium and low-current output harness exit zones along the upper long edge Y=90.0 with OUT3..OUT10 grouped by generic channel number only; DT mated housings remain garage harness parts outside the PCB outline
Engineering decision: Keep DT exits role-free and channel-numbered so hardware does not bind to BMW accessory roles or dedicated functions
Risk: Exact DT boot backshell and fuse-module placement can change spacing; harness labeling must remain generic OUT1..OUT10
Manufacturing boundary: No harness manufacturing drawing connector panel cutout or assembly package is authorized

Layout input: Reserve a user-service cover zone above the output harness side from X25.0..X125.0 and Y72.0..Y90.0 for off-board per-channel fuse access; PCB silkscreen and copper must remain generic OUT1..OUT10; PCB-mounted fuse holders are not selected by this input
Engineering decision: Preserve garage-serviceable fuses as off-board service items while giving future board import a keepout for fuse access and labeling
Risk: MINI versus ATO holder orientation can still move the cover window; field reliability depends on strain relief and fuse retention hardware
Manufacturing boundary: No enclosure service-cover drawing garage harness drawing or PCBA order is authorized

Layout input: Place JPB1 FX18 mezzanine datum at board center X75.0/Y45.0 with long axis along X; use FX18-100P-0.8SV10 plus FX18-100S-0.8SV10 for nominal 20 mm stack; four shared M2.5 NPTH 2.7 mm stack holes at X40/Y25 X110/Y25 X40/Y65 X110/Y65 use 20.3 +/-0.127 mm spacers
Engineering decision: Use a centered four-spacer stack so the fasteners carry vibration load and the connector solder joints remain unloaded
Risk: Physical stack inspection and vibration remain PB-BENCH-014 and PB-BENCH-015 post-prototype requirements
Manufacturing boundary: No PB-100.kicad_pcb LB-100.kicad_pcb drill output or fabrication package is authorized

Layout input: Reserve high-current input and reverse-protection zone X8.0..X45.0/Y15.0..Y75.0; reserve OUT1 and OUT2 MOSFET/shunt/fuse transition zones X35.0..X115.0/Y8.0..Y35.0; reserve OUT3..OUT10 power-stage zones X20.0..X130.0/Y52.0..Y78.0; keep thermal sensors near MOSFET banks without assigning final copper

Risk: Zone allocation can still fail thermal rise or SOA review; copper width via fields and paste segmentation remain thermal/current layout model outputs
Manufacturing boundary: No routed copper Gerbers drills pick-place BOM/CPL manufacturing ZIP or PCBA order is authorized
